name: Amber crib spotlight
headline: Know what happened. Know what’s next.
subhead: A shared baby log that makes the next care moment obvious.
background: warm amber crib-side spotlight, deep cocoa corners, soft bassinet shadow shapes, a small crescent moon glow, premium night-nursery atmosphere
tabBarConstraint: No visible bottom navigation/tab bar; no Today/Care team/Settings bottom labels.
prompt:
Create a pure Nano Banana 2 App Store screenshot concept for BabyRelay using the attached images.

FIRST IMAGE: the real BabyRelay Today app screen. Use it as product truth for the visible BabyRelay UI content.
SECOND IMAGE: the previous "01 Midnight spotlight" style reference. Match its full-screen impact, large product UI, high contrast, and dimensional pop-out treatment, but create a new background concept.

VERY IMPORTANT CHANGE FROM THE REFERENCE: do NOT show the bottom app tab bar/navigation at all. The reference and raw screen may contain bottom navigation, but this new concept must remove it from view. There must be no visible bottom navigation bar, no Today/Care team/Settings labels, and no bottom tab icons anywhere. Achieve this by cropping/framing the phone higher, letting foreground cards cover the lower navigation area, or letting the phone continue below the bottom edge. The visible product UI should focus on the top header, next-up card, asleep button, quick action buttons, and timeline start only.

OUTPUT: one complete 6.9-inch iPhone App Store portrait screenshot concept, roughly 9:19.5 aspect ratio.

HEADLINE TEXT (verbatim): "Know what happened. Know what’s next."
SUBHEAD TEXT (verbatim): "A shared baby log that makes the next care moment obvious."
BACKGROUND DIRECTION: Amber crib spotlight — warm amber crib-side spotlight, deep cocoa corners, soft bassinet shadow shapes, a small crescent moon glow, premium night-nursery atmosphere.

COMPOSITION REQUIREMENTS: Fill the frame like the reference. Make the iPhone/product UI large, bright, and visually prominent. Let the next-up card and quick action buttons pop forward with soft depth. Keep the full concept portrait-shaped, polished, and readable. Keep the headline large and readable at the top.

STRICT CONSTRAINTS: no bottom tab bar/navigation, no Today/Care team/Settings bottom labels, no paywall, no subscription offer, no fake ratings, no fake awards, no App Store badges, no medical claims, no sleep-training promises, no QR code. Do not invent app features beyond the BabyRelay care log/timeline/next-up guidance shown in the raw screen.
